search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG236 stroke follow-up rehabilitation review recommendations
site:nice.org.uk NG128 TIA imaging secondary prevention recommendations
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Acute treatment does not support generic follow-up documentation.
Medicine names and doses were not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: both sources apply to people over 16 or adults, NG236 is stroke-specific, and NG128 primarily addresses initial stroke or TIA management.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for stroke rehabilitation and longer-term review plus specialist TIA imaging and secondary-prevention planning, not a single combined follow-up pathway.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local rehabilitation, imaging, driving, secondary-prevention, and referral pathways.
recency_verification: Current official NG236 and NG128 recommendation pages were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG236 and NG128 remain current official NICE guidance.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped stroke residual, speech, vision, motor, function, mood, swallowing, neurological, gait, speech, power, discharge, and medication-list fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports cognition, vision, hearing, swallowing, movement, continence, communication, nutrition, strength, sensation, balance, mood and mental health, pain, activity, participation, environment, medicines, allergies, goals, discharge information, and documented follow-up planning.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped driving, follow-up, social participation, and longer-term review fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports post-transfer follow-up, information needs, daily activities, participation goals, work, driving and transport discussion, and review of health and social care needs at 6 months and annually.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-decided secondary-prevention documentation after confirmed TIA.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led secondary-prevention planning after specialist confirmation of TIA without mapping medicine names or doses.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed brain and carotid imaging in the specialist TIA context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed MRI and carotid imaging in the specified specialist-assessment context.

unresolved_source_gaps: No single exact source covers the combined stroke or TIA follow-up identity across all populations and settings.

Assessed-negative fields, blood pressure, cardiac findings, lipids, glucose, ECG, adherence, broad risk-factor or lifestyle wording, smoking, generic clinician plans, medicine-specific plan labels, fixed follow-up, and the structured history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, recurrence assessment, treatment selection, urgency, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
